user.title: History Mover
user.description: Add History Mover. It adds a `history_mover.rename` action and a guided rename screen (this card's **Configure** button) that move recorder history from one entity to another.

abort.single_instance_allowed: History Mover is already set up.

single.title: Move history — single
single.description: Move the recorder history of one entity onto another.

data_description.old_entity_id: The id whose history to keep — may be an id no longer in the entity registry.
data_description.new_entity_id: The id that should own the history and keep recording into it.
data_description.on_conflict: What to do if the target already holds history of the same kind.

bulk.title: Move history — bulk
bulk.description: Move every recorder history whose id starts with the source prefix onto the same id with the target prefix. Ids no longer in the registry are included. For arbitrary mappings, use the `history_mover.rename` action with a list.

data_description.on_conflict: What to do if a target already holds history of the same kind.

confirm.title: Confirm
confirm.description:
This changes the recorder database and cannot be undone. Review, then submit to apply.

{summary}

delete.title: Delete history
delete.description: Deletes the complete recorder history (states and long-term statistics) of the selected entities and/or every entity of the selected domains — including ids that no longer exist. Live entities keep their current state and simply start a fresh history. You will see a preview before anything is deleted.

data_description.entity_ids: Exact ids, one per entry, for example `sensor.old_pv_power` — also ids that no longer exist in Home Assistant. Matched exactly as stored in the recorder.
data_description.domains: Whole domains, for example `camera` — deletes the history of every entity of that domain.
data_description.repack: Tries to reclaim the freed disk space by rewriting the entire database file (the same repack as the `recorder.purge` action). Can take a while on a large database.

delete_confirm.title: Confirm
delete_confirm.description:
This deletes the listed histories from the recorder database and cannot be undone. Only the listed ids are deleted — nothing that appears after this preview. Review, then submit to apply.

{summary}

purge.title: Purge orphaned history
purge.description: Finds every recorder history that no existing entity writes into anymore — the id has no current state and no entity registry entry — and deletes its states and statistics. These are typically left over from removed integrations and deleted entities. Disabled entities and external statistics are never touched. You will see a preview before anything is deleted.

data_description.repack: Tries to reclaim the freed disk space by rewriting the entire database file (the same repack as the `recorder.purge` action). Can take a while on a large database.

purge_confirm.title: Confirm
purge_confirm.description:
This deletes the listed histories from the recorder database and cannot be undone. The orphan check is re-run on apply, so an entity that has come back to life since this preview is left untouched. Review, then submit to apply.

{summary}

error.same_id: Source and target must be different.
error.invalid_entity_id: Enter a valid entity ID: lower-case `domain.object_id`, for example `sensor.pv_power`.
error.empty_prefix: Enter a source prefix, for example `sensor.solax_`.
error.invalid_target: This target prefix produces invalid ids — each result must be a lower-case `domain.object_id`.
error.overlapping: The renamed ids would overlap the ids being renamed (the target prefix must not extend the source prefix). Split this into separate operations.
error.no_matches: No recorder history was found for that source prefix.
error.apply_failed: Applying the move failed or timed out. Check the Home Assistant log ('Moved history' entries show what was applied) before submitting again.
error.no_orphans: No orphaned history found — every recorder entry belongs to an existing entity.
error.purge_failed: Applying the purge failed or timed out. Check the Home Assistant log ('Purged orphaned history' entries show what was deleted) before submitting again.
error.empty_selection: Enter at least one entity ID or domain.
error.invalid_domain: Enter plain domains like `sensor` — specific ids belong in the entity IDs field.
error.no_delete_matches: No recorder history matches this selection. IDs are matched exactly as stored — check for typos.
error.delete_failed: Applying the delete failed or timed out. Check the Home Assistant log ('Deleted history' entries show what was deleted) before submitting again.

rename.name: Rename (move history)
rename.description: Moves recorder history (states and long-term statistics) from a source entity id onto a target id. If the target already has history of the same kind, it is discarded and replaced, so a replacement sensor can adopt the old history and keep recording into it.

old_entity_id.name: Source entity ID
old_entity_id.description: The id whose history you want to keep — including an id that no longer exists in the entity registry. Use with 'Target entity ID' for a single rename.

new_entity_id.name: Target entity ID
new_entity_id.description: The id that should own the history from now on. Its own existing history of the same kind is discarded (see 'On conflict').

renames.name: Renames (bulk)
renames.description: A list of pairs, each with old_entity_id and new_entity_id, to move many at once instead of a single pair.

on_conflict.name: On conflict
on_conflict.description: What to do when the target id already holds history of the same kind that the source is about to move onto it.

dry_run.name: Dry run
dry_run.description: Only report what would happen (counts of moved and discarded rows). Nothing is changed. Call with 'Return response' to see the preview.

scan_references.name: Scan references
scan_references.description: After moving, report configuration files that still mention the old id (automations, scripts, scenes, dashboards, UI helpers). Nothing is edited.

delete.name: Delete history
delete.description: Deletes the complete recorder history (states and long-term statistics) of the given entity ids and/or every entity of the given domains — including ids that no longer exist in Home Assistant. Live entities keep their current state and start a fresh history. External statistics (ids with a colon) are never touched. Returns the deleted ids with row counts plus any selection parts that matched nothing; call with 'Return response' and Dry run first to preview.

entity_ids.name: Entity IDs
entity_ids.description: A list of exact ids, for example `sensor.old_pv_power` — also ids that no longer exist. Matched exactly as stored in the recorder.

domains.name: Domains
domains.description: A list of whole domains, for example `camera` — deletes the history of every entity of that domain.

dry_run.name: Dry run
dry_run.description: Only report which histories would be deleted (with row counts). Nothing is changed. Call with 'Return response' to see the preview.

repack.name: Repack
repack.description: After deleting, try to reclaim the freed disk space by rewriting the entire database file (the same repack as the `recorder.purge` action). Can take a while on a large database.

purge_orphans.name: Purge orphaned history
purge_orphans.description: Deletes every recorder history (states and long-term statistics) that no existing entity writes into anymore — the id has no current state and no entity registry entry. Typically left over from removed integrations and deleted entities. Disabled entities and external statistics (ids with a colon, e.g. imported energy data) are never touched. Returns the purged ids with row counts; call with 'Return response' and Dry run first to preview.

dry_run.name: Dry run
dry_run.description: Only report which orphaned histories would be deleted (with row counts). Nothing is changed. Call with 'Return response' to see the preview.

repack.name: Repack
repack.description: After purging, try to reclaim the freed disk space by rewriting the entire database file (the same repack as the `recorder.purge` action). Can take a while on a large database.

options.replace: Replace — discard the target's history, then adopt the source's
options.fail: Fail — report this pair as failed
